rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WALTER J HARRINGTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Against Will Contest
rulings.Rulings: Erishka Harrington's ("Erishka") Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the Alternative, Summary Adjudication Against Will Contest is DENIED. Erishka's objections to the evidence referred to below are overruled. With respect to the undue influence claim, even assuming she may have shown that John Harrington ("John") cannot establish a presumption of undue influence, Erishka fails to shift the burden of production to John because the standard for proving undue influence absent the presumption is flexible and fact intensive and does not depend on the establishment of the three elements of a presumption. (C.C.P. § 437c(p)(2); see David v. Hermann (2005) 129 Cal.App.4th 672, 685-86.) Erishka thus fails to satisfy her initial burden of demonstrating that John cannot present evidence supporting the undue influence claim. Further, even if Erishka had shifted her initial burden, triable issues of material fact exist with respect to Erishka's degree of participation in the estate planning meeting with attorney Kent Olsen (UMF Nos. 9, 12; RUMF Nos. 1-2, 9, 12, AMF No. 3); the degree to which Erishka isolated and asserted control over Walter (AMF Nos. 1-2); whether the 2002 estate plan consituted a significant departure from the 1989 estate plan (UMF Nos. 16-21; RUMF Nos. 10, 16-21) and Walter's motivations for changing his estate plan (UMF Nos. 32-35; RUMF Nos. 10, 23, 37.) Each of those issues (among others) could be considered by the trier of fact as indicia to be weighed in determining whether there was undue influence (see Welf. & Inst. Code section 15610.70(a)) With respect to the claim for forgery, Erishka presented sufficient affirmative evidence in the form of her declaration and the declarations of Kent Olsen and Dawn Jasso to shift the burden of production to John. Based on the declarations of John, Paul, and Mark Harrington, who are qualified to offer testimony regarding the authenticity of Walter's signature on the 2002 Will as persons familiar with his handwriting (Evidence Code § 1416), the authenticity of Walter's signature is a material fact in dispute, making summary adjudication of that claim inappropriate. Prevailing party is directed to submit a form of order consistent with this tentative ruling and compliant with CCP section 437c(g). Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. Tuesday, February 23, 2016.

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: The Motion for Admission of Attorney Pro Hac Vice is continued to March 23, 2016 at 2:30 p.m. so Mr. Melbostad can file an amended motion that satsifies all of the requirements of Cal. Rules of Court, Rule 9.40. The amended motion must include the following: (1) Include attorney Michael Zweig's residence address. (2) State whether Mr. Zweig is currently disbarred from any court. (3) Address whether Mr. Zweig has ever in the last two years applied to appear pro hac vice in California. (4) Include proof of service of notice on all parties who have appeared in the case as well as proof of service on the State Bar of California (CRC, Rule 9.40(c)(1); C.C.P. 1005(b)).
